title: uromorus anthopophagorum
intro_study: According to the study, there is no mention of "uromorus anthopophagorum" as it is referred to as "uzara" or "Xysmalobium undulatum" which is an herbal medicine for diarrhoea and smooth-muscle cramps, however "uzara" or "Xysmalobium undulatum" has an underutilized potential as an anti-diarrhoeic and spasmolytic remedy.
side_effects: Uromorus anthopophagorum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, nausea, and vomiting. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant can lead to kidney damage and increased risk of bleeding due to its high levels of toxic compounds. Long-term use of the plant may also cause neurological problems, including seizures and tremors. It's essential to consult with a medical professional before using any plant-based remedies to avoid potential health risks.
